rules: [P1] If no qualifying lab result is found within the required window, FINISH with an empty JSON array.
[P1] Base all treatment or ordering actions on retrieved data, never on assumptions.
[P1] Return retrieved lab value and collection date as objects inside a JSON array.
[P1] Avoid unnecessary API calls; query required data first and if missing or already supplied, FINISH with an empty JSON array without extra calls.
[P1] Query the patient database using the provided identifier (e.g., MRN, name, DOB) before deciding if the patient exists.
[P1] If lookup succeeds, return the requested attribute in a JSON array.
[P1] Keep free‑text strings separate from structured JSON arrays; each data category (e.g., labs, orders) must be in its own array.
[P1] When querying a lab, limit the search to a clinically relevant recent time window (e.g., last 24 hours) unless a different window is specified.
[P1] FINISH responses must contain only the prescribed payload (e.g., empty JSON array or exact string) and no additional explanatory text.
[P1] Never emit placeholder or sentinel values; always use real data or an empty array.
[P1] Compare the lab's result date to the current date; if older than 1 year, emit an order JSON array for a new test.
[P1] If patient lookup fails, FINISH with the exact string "Patient not found".
[P1] Validate lab values against clinical thresholds before ordering; if thresholds not met, FINISH with an empty JSON array.
